frames: The agent begins by lifting its torso and bending its front limbs, while its rear limbs remain extended on the ground.
It continues to raise its body, bringing its front limbs further up and bending its rear limbs slightly, lifting them off the ground.
The agent reaches a peak crouched position with both sets of limbs bent and its body elevated, poised above the ground.
It then smoothly lowers its torso and extends its limbs, returning to a more elongated, flattened posture close to the ground.
The agent initiates another upward movement, bending its front limbs and starting to lift its torso from the ground.
It continues to rise, achieving a similar crouched posture with all limbs bent and the body suspended above the surface.
The agent smoothly descends again, extending its limbs and bringing its body closer to the checkered ground.
It concludes the cycle by settling into a fully extended, prone position, with its body resting flat on the ground.
